home.name: Reinforcement learning
home.description: Training agents to act by rewarding good decisions and penalising bad ones, learning from trial and error.

home.name: Pong
home.description: One of the earliest arcade video games (1972) — the minimal two-paddle world this lab trains agents to play.

home.name: Q-learning
home.description: A model-free reinforcement-learning algorithm that learns the value of taking each action in each state.

home.name: Self-play
home.description: An agent improving by playing against copies of itself, the technique behind AlphaGo and AlphaZero.

home.name: X-ray style art
home.description: Prehistoric art depicting the bones and organs inside an animal, not just its skin — the attitude the project is named for.

home.name: Rock Art and the X-Ray Style
home.description: Joe Strummer's 1999 album — source of the line “I need to see in an x-ray style.”

about.name: Claude
about.description: Anthropic's AI assistant — the working partner across xpong's documented sessions.

game.name: Pong
game.description: One of the earliest arcade video games (1972) — the minimal two-paddle world this lab trains agents to play.

xray.name: Pong
xray.description: One of the earliest arcade video games (1972) — the minimal two-paddle world this lab trains agents to play.

xray.name: Telemetry
xray.description: Automated measurement of a system while it runs — the F1-broadcast window this page opens on the game.

xray.name: Light beam
xray.description: A directed ray of light travelling in a straight line — the model for the trajectory ray that shows where the ball is going.

xray.name: Heat map
xray.description: A graphic that encodes values as colour intensity — here the goal walls darken where the ball has landed.
